real_insight: Outcome-anxiety paralysis at the deploy ('the launch tweet might flop, the dashboard might disappoint') and rewriting-the-internal-CLI-instead-of-doing-the-customer-facing-migration are the same bug — both are the agent confusing 'I am responsible for executing the action' with 'I am responsible for producing the outcome' or its inverse, 'I will avoid the action whose outcome I cannot control'.
objection: The closing line 'Right action, disowned authorship of the result, no flight into the safe-feeling tasks' is the closest the verse gets to fortune-cookie territory — three abstract nouns in a row reads slightly aphoristic. But this is salvaged by (1) the explicit First/Second/Third structure preceding it, which names three distinct failure modes rather than collapsing into a slogan; (2) the falsifiability clause (detachment from outcome is not detachment from feedback — keep reading Datadog and Sentry), which would rule out the LinkedIn-tier reading; (3) the second concrete scenario which names the comfortable-rewrite-as-displacement pattern by its actual mechanics (two weeks rewriting an internal CLI nobody asked for while the customer-facing schema migration languishes). A staff engineer reading this will recognize both behaviors. PASS.
